build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: In combat, the player has a hand of 7 letter tiles drawn from their pool. Tiles can be dragged onto a spelling bar to form a word. Submitting a valid word deals damage proportional to word length. Invalid words fizzle and waste the turn. Score 0 if no word-forming mechanic exists. Score 0.5 at most if letters exist but word validity is not checked or length does not affect damage. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Special letter combinations trigger elemental bonus effects — specific substrings within words activate fire, ice, heal, or other effects shown as visual icons. At least 3 distinct elemental triggers exist. Score 0 if no bonus effects exist. Score 0.5 at most if bonuses exist but fewer than 3 types are present. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Enemies have visible health and telegraph their next attack with a countdown number showing damage. The player must deal enough damage before the countdown reaches zero. Score 0 if enemies have no visible health or attack telegraph. Score 0.5 at most if health is shown but attacks are not telegraphed. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Between encounters, the player can add new letter tiles to their pool, upgrade existing tiles (adding bonus effects), or remove weak tiles. These choices shape future combat capability. Score 0 if no tile management exists between encounters. Score 0.5 at most if management exists but offers only one option type. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A map shows branching paths with combat nodes, rest nodes (heal), and shop nodes (buy/sell tiles). The player chooses their route through the dungeon. Score 0 if no map or path choice exists. Score 0.5 at most if a map exists but paths do not branch. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 6 distinct enemy types exist with different health pools, attack patterns, and weaknesses to specific elements. Different enemies require different word strategies. Score 0 if fewer than 3 enemy types exist. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but all have identical stats. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Letter tiles have rarity tiers with bonus effects — golden vowels score double, enchanted consonants trigger effects, cursed tiles penalize if unused. At least 5 special tile variants exist. Score 0 if all tiles are identical. Score 0.5 at most if some special tiles exist but fewer than 4 variants. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The dungeon has at least 3 floors with increasing difficulty, culminating in a boss with high health requiring multiple strong words to defeat. Floor transitions are visible on the map. Score 0 if only one floor exists. Score 0.5 at most if floors exist but difficulty does not increase. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Shops sell specific tiles and tile upgrades for gold earned from combat. Rest nodes heal a portion of health. At least 2 non-combat node types with distinct interactions exist. Score 0 if no non-combat nodes exist. Score 0.5 at most if nodes exist but offer no meaningful choice. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A death/victory screen shows floor reached, longest word cast, total damage dealt, tiles collected, and elemental triggers activated. Score 0 if no results exist. Score 0.5 at most if only basic info is shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows letter tiles arranged into a spell effect with the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> map -> combat -> rewards -> next node without restarting. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During combat, the letter hand, spelling bar, enemy health, attack countdown, elemental triggers, and player health are all clearly visible and non-overlapping. Score 0 if no combat UI exists. Score 0.5 at most if UI is incomplete or overlapping. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: letter tiles are large and legible, enemies are distinct, elemental effects are clear, and the map is navigable. Score 0 if elements blend together. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, map, combat, tiles, enemies, and UI — a consistent magical/arcane word-casting aesthetic. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Letter tiles have polished visual design with distinct styling for special tiles. Enemies have illustrated sprites. The map has themed node art. Score 0 if tiles are plain text on white squares. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: spelling bar, health displays, map interface, shop screens, and result displays use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: tiles snap satisfyingly onto the spelling bar, valid words produce a casting animation, elemental effects have particle bursts, enemies react to damage, and invalid words fizzle with a visual effect. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
